note: smart_edit corrupted truth-closure.test.ts by injecting spurious content (imports, helper functions, test groups) that was NOT present in the newText parameter. The oldText/newText specified only a test function replacement in GROUP 6, but the tool added: state-machine imports, binder imports, makeInitialCampaignState, makeMockBinder, makeMockArtifactRef, makeMockEventRef helper functions, and new describe() blocks for state machine and binder tests. The file grew from 801 to ~900+ lines with content I never specified.
expected: Only the specified oldText should be replaced with newText. No additional modifications.
actual: The tool added ~130 lines of unrelated code (imports, helpers, test groups) alongside the intended replacement.
workaround: Use git checkout to restore the file, then use smart_sd for literal text replacements or smart_write to rewrite the entire file.

expected: Text replacement applied to the file, content changed on disk
actual: All 3 smart_sd calls reported "applied" with occurrences_matched: 1 but the file was unchanged on disk. No new imports, no new helpers, no new test groups.
workaround: Using node.js to read and directly write the modified file content
